title: Air-fryer hasselback potatoes
subtitle: Fan-cut potatoes with crisp edges and a soft centre
excerpt: Medium potatoes cut with closely spaced slices to three-quarters depth, brushed with garlic oil, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 35 to 40 minutes. The fanned cut gives each potato a large surface area for crisping without losing the soft, steamed interior the whole potato provides.
sourceNotes: Hasselback potatoes are named after the Hasselbacken restaurant in Stockholm, which popularised the style in the 1950s. The technique spread through European cooking through the 1970s and has had several revivals as a dinner-party side dish. The narrow cuts open during roasting to produce crisp edges on a soft, steamed interior.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Score and oil the potatoes up to 2 hours ahead. Refrigerate uncovered until ready to cook.

content.text: Place a chopstick on each side of the potato before cutting. The chopsticks act as a depth stop: the knife hits them before cutting all the way through. Without this guide, it is very easy to slice through the base and lose the fan effect entirely. Cut the slices 3 mm apart, working your way along the length of the potato.

items.prepNote: medium Maris Piper or King Edward, about 200g each

content.text: Scrub the {{potato}} and dry thoroughly. Place each potato between two chopsticks on a board. Cut slices 3 mm apart across the top, cutting down until the knife touches the chopsticks. Mix the melted {{unsalted-butter}}, {{garlic}}, {{rosemary}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Brush all over the potatoes, working the butter into the slices with the brush. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Place the potatoes cut-side up in the basket. Cook at 200°C for 20 minutes, brush again with any remaining butter, then cook for a further 15 to 20 minutes until the edges of the slices are golden and crisp and a knife slides easily into the centre.

items.symptom: Slices not opening up or fanning during cooking
items.cause: Slices cut too far apart, or potato too small
items.fix: Cuts at 3 mm intervals open properly as the potato steams from the inside. Cuts at 5 to 10 mm do not fan. Use a medium potato: small ones (under 150g) do not have enough space between cuts to fan visibly

items.symptom: Potato cut all the way through into separate pieces
items.cause: Chopstick guides not used, or removed before finishing
items.fix: Keep the chopsticks in place for the full length of cutting. If a potato has been sliced through, it can still be cooked but will need to be handled carefully as a fan rather than a whole potato

items.symptom: Outside browned but centre still firm after 40 minutes
items.cause: Potato too large, or mid-cook basting skipped
items.fix: A 200g potato takes 35 to 40 minutes. Larger potatoes (over 250g) may need 45 minutes. The second basting at 20 minutes helps the centre cook by transferring additional heat into the slices

content.text: Hasselback potatoes are a dinner-party side that looks involved but requires only a sharp knife and patience. Serve alongside a roast chicken, a steak, or a baked salmon fillet. In the last 5 minutes of cooking, a tablespoon of grated Parmesan pressed into the open slices melts between them and adds a crisp, salty finish.
